merged_findings.finding:
The adversarial review is complete. The output above is the full JSON array of findings — 1 blocker, 7 warnings, and 4 notes.

**The critical blocker**: Phase 5 introduces `partial_pass` to a Literal type and `LocalTestResult` as a new dataclass, but Phase 1's contract states all model fields are declared up-front and no later phase adds new fields/variants. Phase 5 isn't listed as modifying `models.py`, so this will either require a Phase 1 amendment or a Phase 5 file-list correction before bui

claude.finding:
The adversarial review is complete. The output above is the full JSON array of findings — 1 blocker, 7 warnings, and 4 notes.

**The critical blocker**: Phase 5 introduces `partial_pass` to a Literal type and `LocalTestResult` as a new dataclass, but Phase 1's contract states all model fields are declared up-front and no later phase adds new fields/variants. Phase 5 isn't listed as modifying `models.py`, so this will either require a Phase 1 amendment or a Phase 5 file-list correction before bui

merged_findings.finding: Phase 1 makes `deps.py` run hard preflight checks at startup (`OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, live OpenRouter call, cluster scripts), but the same phase’s success criteria require `br benchmark --help` to work. If preflight is wired into CLI startup rather than only execution paths, `--help` will fail in any environment missing cluster/OpenRouter access.

codex.finding: Phase 1 makes `deps.py` run hard preflight checks at startup (`OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, live OpenRouter call, cluster scripts), but the same phase’s success criteria require `br benchmark --help` to work. If preflight is wired into CLI startup rather than only execution paths, `--help` will fail in any environment missing cluster/OpenRouter access.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 1 requires `python -m core.benchmark.cli --help` to return 0, but the plan never specifies a module entrypoint in `core/benchmark/cli.py` (for example calling the Typer app under `if __name__ == "__main__":`). Creating a Typer app object alone does not make `python -m core.benchmark.cli` execute the CLI.

codex.finding: Phase 1 requires `python -m core.benchmark.cli --help` to return 0, but the plan never specifies a module entrypoint in `core/benchmark/cli.py` (for example calling the Typer app under `if __name__ == "__main__":`). Creating a Typer app object alone does not make `python -m core.benchmark.cli` execute the CLI.

merged_findings.finding: Phase replacement is underspecified and internally inconsistent: Phase 3/4 call `selector.redraw(phase_id)` from per-cell failure paths (`run_cell` / paraphrase failure), but the benchmark is phase-balanced across all models, sub-studies, and replications. Triggering redraw from a single-cell path can schedule duplicate replacements for the same dropped phase or drop N without ever enqueuing the replacement phase across the full cell matrix.

codex.finding: Phase replacement is underspecified and internally inconsistent: Phase 3/4 call `selector.redraw(phase_id)` from per-cell failure paths (`run_cell` / paraphrase failure), but the benchmark is phase-balanced across all models, sub-studies, and replications. Triggering redraw from a single-cell path can schedule duplicate replacements for the same dropped phase or drop N without ever enqueuing the replacement phase across the full cell matrix.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 5’s Python test setup assumes all needed dependencies come from root `requirements*.txt`, then does `pip install --no-deps -e <clone_path>` when `pyproject.toml` exists. The repo tree shows `pyproject.toml` is present. Any dependency declared only in `pyproject.toml` and not duplicated in a root requirements file will be missing in `.venv-benchmark/`, causing import/test failures in fresh clones.

codex.finding: Phase 5’s Python test setup assumes all needed dependencies come from root `requirements*.txt`, then does `pip install --no-deps -e <clone_path>` when `pyproject.toml` exists. The repo tree shows `pyproject.toml` is present. Any dependency declared only in `pyproject.toml` and not duplicated in a root requirements file will be missing in `.venv-benchmark/`, causing import/test failures in fresh clones.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 10 has conflicting completion requirements: one deliverable says `br benchmark run --phases 30 ...` completes all 360 runs, while the same phase also allows `selector` to return fewer than 30 phases and defers to degraded-sample rules when achieved N < 30. Those two success conditions cannot both be true for the same run.

codex.finding: Phase 10 has conflicting completion requirements: one deliverable says `br benchmark run --phases 30 ...` completes all 360 runs, while the same phase also allows `selector` to return fewer than 30 phases and defers to degraded-sample rules when achieved N < 30.
